components.name: dipotassium hydrogen phosphate (K2HPO4)
components.evidence: Tweeddale, Notley-McRobb & Ferenci 1998 (same lab, same glucose-limited chemostat system): 'Minimal medium A (MMA) (28) was the basal salts medium for both chemostat and batch cultures.' MMA (Miller, Experiments in Molecular Genetics, 1972) per litre 1x: K2HPO4 10.5 g, KH2PO4 4.5 g, (NH4)2SO4 1 g, sodium citrate.2H2O 0.5 g.

components.name: potassium dihydrogen phosphate (KH2PO4)
components.evidence: MMA (Miller 1972) per litre 1x: KH2PO4 4.5 g. Confirmed as the Ferenci-lab basal salts medium in Tweeddale et al. 1998: 'Minimal medium A (MMA) (28) was the basal salts medium for both chemostat and batch cultures.'

components.name: ammonium sulfate ((NH4)2SO4)
components.evidence: MMA (Miller 1972) per litre 1x: (NH4)2SO4 1 g. Full 1 g/L applies under O-limitation; PMC5464527 states only the N-limited variant reduces it: 'The N-limited medium ... contained the same basal medium as the P-limited culture but with (NH4)2SO4 reduced to 0.03 g/l', implying the standard medium carries the full ammonium concentration.

components.name: trisodium citrate dihydrate (sodium citrate)
components.evidence: PMC5464527 Methods: 'An Fe-depleted environment was obtained by removing sodium citrate and Fe2SO4 from the medium as described in [76]' -- i.e. sodium citrate is present in all non-Fe-limited media (including O-limited). MMA (Miller 1972) contains sodium citrate.2H2O 0.5 g/L.

components.name: magnesium sulfate heptahydrate (MgSO4.7H2O)
components.concentration: 1 mM (1 ml of 1 M per L)
components.evidence: MMA (Miller 1972) 1x additions per litre: '1 ml 1 M MgSO4.7H2O ... added per liter when diluted to 1x'.

components.name: thiamine (vitamin B1)
components.evidence: PMC5464527 GrowthDB record lists thiamine (vitamin B1) as a defined additive. MMA (Miller 1972) 1x additions per litre: '0.1 ml 0.5% vitamin B1 (thiamine) added per liter when diluted to 1x'.

components.name: ferrous sulfate (FeSO4; written 'Fe2SO4' in paper)
components.evidence: PMC5464527 Methods: 'An Fe-depleted environment was obtained by removing sodium citrate and Fe2SO4 from the medium as described in [76]' -- FeSO4 is therefore a component of all non-Fe-limited media, including the O-limited medium. Exact concentration not given in the paper or the accessible base reference (Schliep, Ryall & Ferenci 2012, Mol. Biosyst.).

components.name: D-glucose
components.evidence: 'For O-limited chemostats, the airflow through the sparger was blocked to create an oxygen-depleted environment and supplemented with 0.08% glucose as described previously [77].' Higher glucose (vs 0.02% for C-limited) is deliberately supplied so glucose is in excess and oxygen is the sole limitation.

media.base_medium: Minimal medium A (MMA) of Miller 1972 (Experiments in Molecular Genetics) -- the Ferenci-lab basal salts medium; here the MMA-based chemostat medium of Schliep, Ryall & Ferenci 2012, supplemented with 0.08% glucose and run with the sparger airflow blocked (oxygen-limited).

sources_chased.what: PMC5464527 Methods (base medium = ref 76 Schliep 2012 + 0.08% glucose; O-limited protocol = ref 77 Manche 1999)
added: FeSO4 + sodium citrate present in non-Fe-limited media

sources_chased.what: ref 76 Schliep, Ryall & Ferenci 2012, Mol. Biosyst. (base chemostat minimal medium)
sources_chased.how: WebFetch doi.org/10.1039/c2mb05495g redirected to paywalled OUP; Methods not readable

sources_chased.what: ref 77 Manche, Notley-McRobb & Ferenci 1999, Genetics 153:5 (PMC1460742) -- aerobic vs O-limited glucose-limited chemostats
sources_chased.how: WebFetch PMC page returned abstract + refs only (Methods behind PDF)

sources_chased.what: Tweeddale, Notley-McRobb & Ferenci 1998, J. Bacteriol. 180:5109 (PMC107546) -- same lab, same glucose-limited chemostat system: identifies basal medium
added: basal salts medium = Minimal medium A (MMA), Miller 1972, ref 28

sources_chased.what: Miller 1972 Minimal Medium A composition (K2HPO4/KH2PO4/(NH4)2SO4/citrate + Mg/glucose/thiamine)
sources_chased.how: WebSearch (Rice Bennett Lab MinA wiki / iGEM HKUST-Rice recipe reproduce Miller MinA)

missing_before: ammonium sulfate 1.0 g/L (was only a generic NH4 exchange)
media.notes: O-limited = oxygen-depleted (sparger airflow blocked) glucose-excess chemostat of E. coli MC4100; not a strictly reduced/anaerobic medium -- no cysteine/sulfide reductant is added and no defined gas mix is used, so 'anaerobic' here means O2-limited/fermentative. The base is Miller (1972) Minimal Medium A (confirmed as the Ferenci-lab basal salts medium in Tweeddale et al. 1998 and used for this paper's plates), plus FeSO4/citrate and thiamine, supplemented with 0.08% glucose. Note: Miller MMA contains NO trace-element solution, so the Zn2+, Mn2+, Cu2+, Co2+ (and Ca2+, Cl-) exchanges in the current GEM listing are model-default open uptakes, NOT defined recipe ingredients -- they have no textual source and were not added as sourced components. The exact FeSO4 concentration lives in the paywalled Schliep 2012 base reference and could not be recovered.
